name: Panorama
description: This app integrates with the Palo Alto Networks Panorama product to support several containment and investigative actions

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: URL to block

parameters.name: device_group
parameters.description: Device group in which you want to block url

parameters.name: policy_type
parameters.description: Block policy type

parameters.name: policy_name
parameters.description: Policy to use

parameters.name: audit_comment
parameters.description: Audit comment to be used with the policy name. Maximum 256 characters

parameters.name: should_commit_changes
parameters.description: Whether to commit both, changes to firewall and changes to device groups at the end of this action

parameters.name: use_partial_commit
parameters.description: Whether to perform user specific commit. As part of the request, the configuration's username is included as the administrator name (when the 'should_commit_changes' is 'false' the 'use_partial_commit' parameter is ignored)

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: URL to unblock

parameters.name: device_group
parameters.description: Device group in which you want to unblock url

parameters.name: should_commit_changes
parameters.description: Whether to commit both, changes to firewall and changes to device groups at the end of this action

parameters.name: use_partial_commit
parameters.description: Whether to perform user specific commit. As part of the request, the configuration's username is included as the administrator name (when the 'should_commit_changes' is 'false' the 'use_partial_commit' parameter is ignored)

parameters.description: Application to block

parameters.description: Device group in which you want to block application

parameters.name: use_partial_commit

actions.name: List Applications
actions.description: List the applications that the device knows about and can block

parameters.name: log_type
parameters.description: Log type to query

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Query to run

parameters.name: range
parameters.description: Range of result logs to retrieve (e.g 1-5000 or 100-700)

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Direction to search

actions.name: Commit Changes
actions.description: Commit changes to the firewall and device groups

parameters.name: device_group
parameters.description: Device group whose changes you want to push to firewall

parameters.name: use_partial_commit
parameters.description: Whether to perform user specific commit. As part of the request, the configuration's username is included as the administrator name (when the 'should_commit_changes' is 'false' the 'use_partial_commit' parameter is ignored)

parameters.name: pcap_id
parameters.description: PCAP ID required to fetch Threat PCAP

parameters.name: device_name
parameters.description: Device Name required to fetch Threat PCAP

parameters.name: session_id
parameters.description: Session ID required to fetch Threat PCAP

parameters.name: search_time
parameters.description: Search time that the Threat PCAP was received on the firewall (yyyy/mm/dd hr:min:sec)

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: Filename of exported PCAP file

parameters.name: device_group
parameters.description: Device group whose edl you want to list (up to 31 characters, default is 'shared')

actions.name: Get Edl
actions.description: Get data of an External Dynamic List

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name of the external dynamic list you want to get data off (up to 63 characters)

parameters.name: device_group
parameters.description: Device group in which the edl exists (up to 31 characters, default is 'shared')

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name of the external dynamic list you want to create (up to 63 characters)

parameters.name: device_group
parameters.description: Device group in which you want to create the edl (up to 31 characters, default is 'shared')

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Description of external dynamic list (max char : 255)

parameters.name: list_type
parameters.description: Type of external dynamic list

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Source url to fetch the data

parameters.name: exception_list
parameters.description: List of exceptions (comma separated values)

parameters.name: expand_for_subdomains
parameters.description: Expand to include subdomains of a specified domain automatically (only used when list_type is Domain list)

parameters.name: disable_override
parameters.description: Used to ensure that a firewall administrator cannot override settings locally on a firewall that inherits this configuration through a Device Group commit from Panorama (only used when device group is not 'shared')

parameters.name: certificate_profile
parameters.description: Certificate profile is used for authenticating the server that hosts the list (only used when list_type is not predefined IP or URL)

parameters.name: check_for_updates
parameters.description: Defines the frequency at which the firewall retrieves the list  (only used when list_type is not predefined IP or URL)

parameters.name: hour
parameters.description: At what hour of the day to check for updates  (only used when check_for_update type is daily, weekly or monthly)

parameters.name: day_of_week
parameters.description: On which specific day of week to check for updates (only used when check_for_update type is weekly)

parameters.name: day_of_month
parameters.description: On which specific date of month to check for updates (only used when check_for_update type is monthly)

parameters.name: should_commit_changes
parameters.description: Whether to commit both, changes to firewall and changes to device groups at the end of this action

parameters.name: use_partial_commit
parameters.description: Whether to perform user specific commit. As part of the request, the configuration's username is included as the administrator name (when the 'should_commit_changes' is 'false' the 'use_partial_commit' parameter is ignored)

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name of the external dynamic list you want to modify (up to 63 characters)

parameters.name: device_group
parameters.description: Device group in which the edl exists (up to 31 characters, default is 'shared')

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Description of external dynamic list (max char : 255)

parameters.name: list_type
parameters.description: Type of external dynamic list

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Source url to fetch the data

parameters.name: exception_list
parameters.description: List of exceptions (comma separated values)

parameters.name: expand_for_subdomains
parameters.description: Expand to include subdomains of a specified domain automatically (only used when list_type is Domain list)

parameters.name: disable_override
parameters.description: Used to ensure that a firewall administrator cannot override settings locally on a firewall that inherits this configuration through a Device Group commit from Panorama (only used when device group is not 'shared')

parameters.name: certificate_profile
parameters.description: Certificate profile is used for authenticating the server that hosts the list (only used when list_type is not predefined IP or URL)

parameters.name: check_for_updates
parameters.description: Defines the frequency at which the firewall retrieves the list  (only used when list_type is not predefined IP or URL)

parameters.name: hour
parameters.description: At what hour of the day to check for updates (only used when check_for_update type is daily, weekly or monthly)

parameters.name: day_of_week
parameters.description: On which specific day of week to check for updates (only used when check_for_update type is weekly)

parameters.name: day_of_month
parameters.description: On which specific date of month to check for updates (only used when check_for_update type is monthly)

parameters.name: should_commit_changes
parameters.description: Whether to commit both, changes to firewall and changes to device groups at the end of this action

parameters.name: use_partial_commit
parameters.description: Whether to perform user specific commit. As part of the request, the configuration's username is included as the administrator name (when the 'should_commit_changes' is 'false' the 'use_partial_commit' parameter is ignored)

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name of the external dynamic list you want to delete (up to 63 characters)

parameters.name: device_group
parameters.description: Device group in which the edl exists (up to 31 characters, default is 'shared')

parameters.name: should_commit_changes
parameters.description: Whether to commit both, changes to firewall and changes to device groups at the end of this action

parameters.name: use_partial_commit
parameters.description: Whether to perform user specific commit. As part of the request, the configuration's username is included as the administrator name (when the 'should_commit_changes' is 'false' the 'use_partial_commit' parameter is ignored)

parameters.name: policy_name
parameters.description: Name of the security policy rule (max length is 63 characters, which can be letters, numbers, spaces, hyphens, dot and underscores)

parameters.name: device_group
parameters.description: Device group to create the policy rule in (up to 31 characters, default is 'shared')

parameters.name: policy_type
parameters.description: Rule base to create the policy in (pre-rule or post-rule)

parameters.name: rule_type
parameters.description: Rule type of the policy rule (specifies whether the rule applies to traffic within a zone, between zones, or both)

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Description for the policy (max length 1024 characters)

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: List of tags(comma separated) to apply to this policy (allow to group objects using keywords or phrases, max length 127 characters)

parameters.name: audit_comment
parameters.description: Describe the changes made and why the rule was created by adding an audit comment(once the configuration is commited, it would be reflected in Audit Comment Archive for future reference)

parameters.name: source_zone
parameters.description: Source zones for policy (default is Any)

parameters.name: source_address
parameters.description: List of source addresses, address groups, or regions (comma separated, default is Any) to be added to the policy

parameters.name: negate_source
parameters.description: Whether to negate the source (apply the rule to source addresses from the specified zone except for the addresses specified)

parameters.name: source_user
parameters.description: List of source users or groups of users (comma separated) subject to the policy

parameters.name: source_device
parameters.description: List of host devices(comma separated) subject to the policy

parameters.name: destination_zone
parameters.description: Destinations zones(comma separated) for policy (default is Any)

parameters.name: destination_device
parameters.description: List of host devices(comma separated) subject to the policy

parameters.name: destination_address
parameters.description: List of destination addresses, address groups, or regions (comma separated, default is Any) to be added to the policy

parameters.name: negate_destination
parameters.description: Whether to negate the destination (apply the rule to destination addresses from the specified zone except for the addresses specified)

parameters.name: application
parameters.description: List of specific applications(comma separated) for the security policy rule

parameters.name: service
parameters.description: List of services(comma separated) of the policy (services to limit to specific TCP or UDP port numbers)

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: List of URL Categories(comma separated) of the policy

parameters.name: profile_setting
parameters.description: Type of profile setting to choose for the policy (additional checking that the firewall performs on packets that match the Security profile rule)

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Action the firewall takes on traffic that matches the attributes defined in a rule

parameters.name: icmp_unreachable
parameters.description: Whether to send information to the client that a session is not allowed. Applicable only in case action is 'Drop', 'Reset Client', 'Reset Server', 'Reset Both'

parameters.name: log_forwarding
parameters.description: To forward the local traffic log and threat log entries to remote destinations, such as Panorama

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: Apply the rule to specific firewalls or descendant device groups of the Device Group (or Shared location) where the rule is defined (Takes serial number of firewall as input)

parameters.name: where
parameters.description: Where to position the policy, by default the policy would be created at the bottom

parameters.name: dst
parameters.description: Policy in reference to which, position the current policy (only applicable when input for 'where' is 'after' or 'before')

parameters.name: disable
parameters.description: Whether to disable the policy

parameters.name: should_commit_changes
parameters.description: Whether to commit both, changes to firewall and changes to device groups at the end of this action

parameters.name: use_partial_commit
parameters.description: Whether to perform user specific commit. As part of the request, the configuration's username is included as the administrator name (when the 'should_commit_changes' is 'false' the 'use_partial_commit' parameter is ignored)

actions.name: Custom Block Policy
actions.description: Block IP addresses, Address Groups, EDLs(External Dynamic List), Applications, or URL Categories in Panorama and creates a custom uni-directional (direction parameter value as from or to) or bi-directional (direction parameter value as both) security rule

parameters.name: policy_name
parameters.description: Name of the security policy rule (max length is 63 characters, which can be letters, numbers, spaces, hyphens, dot and underscores)

parameters.name: device_group
parameters.description: Device group to create the policy rule in (up to 31 characters, default is 'shared')

parameters.name: policy_type
parameters.description: Rule base to create the policy in (pre-rule or post-rule)

parameters.name: rule_type
parameters.description: Rule type of the policy rule (specifies whether the rule applies to traffic within a zone, between zones, or both)

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Description for the policy (max length 1024 characters)

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: List of tags to apply to this policy (allow to group objects using keywords or phrases, max length 127 characters)

parameters.name: audit_comment
parameters.description: Describe the changes made and why the rule was created by adding an audit comment(once the configuration is commited, it would be reflected in Audit Comment Archive for future reference)

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Direction to block the traffic (Default is 'both')

parameters.name: object_type
parameters.description: Type of object to block

parameters.name: object_value
parameters.description: Value of the object to be blocked. Can be a list(comma separated)

parameters.name: icmp_unreachable
parameters.description: Whether to send information to the client that a session is not allowed. Applicable only in case action is 'Drop', 'Reset Client', 'Reset Server', 'Reset Both'

parameters.name: log_forwarding
parameters.description: To forward the local traffic log and threat log entries to remote destinations, such as Panorama

parameters.name: where
parameters.description: Where to position the policy, by default the policy would be created at the bottom

parameters.name: dst
parameters.description: Policy in reference to which, position the current policy

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: Apply the rule to specific firewalls or descendant device groups of the Device Group (or Shared location) where the rule is defined (Takes serial number of firewall as input)

parameters.name: should_commit_changes
parameters.description: Whether to commit both, changes to firewall and changes to device groups at the end of this action

parameters.name: use_partial_commit
parameters.description: Whether to perform user specific commit. As part of the request, the configuration's username is included as the administrator name (when the 'should_commit_changes' is 'false' the 'use_partial_commit' parameter is ignored)

parameters.name: policy_name
parameters.description: Name of the security policy rule

parameters.name: device_group
parameters.description: Device group of the policy rule (default is 'shared')

parameters.name: policy_type
parameters.description: Rule base of the policy (pre-rule or post-rule)

parameters.name: rule_type
parameters.description: Rule type of the policy rule (specifies whether the rule applies to traffic within a zone, between zones, or both)

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Description for the policy (max length 1024 characters)

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: List of tags(comma separated) to apply to this policy (allow to group objects using keywords or phrases, max length 127 characters)

parameters.name: audit_comment
parameters.description: Describe the changes made and why the rule was created by adding an audit comment(once the configuration is commited, it would be reflected in Audit Comment Archive for future reference)

parameters.name: source_zone
parameters.description: List of source zones(comma separated) for policy (default is Any)

parameters.name: source_address
parameters.description: List of source addresses, address groups, or regions (comma separated, default is Any) to be added to the policy

parameters.name: negate_source
parameters.description: Whether to negate the source (apply the rule to source addresses from the specified zone except for the addresses specified)

parameters.name: source_user
parameters.description: List of source users or groups of users(comma separated) subject to the policy

parameters.name: source_device
parameters.description: The host devices(comma separated) subject to the policy

parameters.name: destination_zone
parameters.description: List of destinations zones(comma separated) for policy (default is Any)

parameters.name: destination_device
parameters.description: List of host devices(comma separated) subject to the policy

parameters.name: destination_address
parameters.description: List of destination addresses, address groups, or regions (default is Any) to be added to the policy

parameters.name: negate_destination
parameters.description: Whether to negate the destination (apply the rule to destination addresses from the specified zone except for the addresses specified)

parameters.name: application
parameters.description: List of specific applications(comma separated) for the Security policy rule

parameters.name: service
parameters.description: List of services of the policy (comma separated, services to limit to specific TCP or UDP port numbers)

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: List of URL Categories(comma separated) of the policy

parameters.name: profile_setting
parameters.description: Type of profile setting to choose for the policy (additional checking that the firewall performs on packets that match the Security profile rule)

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Action the firewall takes on traffic that matches the attributes defined in a rule

parameters.name: icmp_unreachable
parameters.description: Whether to send sent information to the client that a session is not allowed. Applicable only in case action is 'Drop', 'Reset Client', 'Reset Server', 'Reset Both'

parameters.name: log_forwarding
parameters.description: To forward the local traffic log and threat log entries to remote destinations, such as Panorama

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: Apply the rule to specific firewalls or descendant device groups of the Device Group (or Shared location) where the rule is defined (Takes serial number of firewall as input)

parameters.name: disable
parameters.description: Whether to disable the policy

parameters.name: should_commit_changes
parameters.description: Whether to commit both, changes to firewall and changes to device groups at the end of this action

parameters.name: use_partial_commit
parameters.description: Whether to perform user specific commit. As part of the request, the configuration's username is included as the administrator name (when the 'should_commit_changes' is 'false' the 'use_partial_commit' parameter is ignored)

parameters.name: policy_name
parameters.description: Name of the security policy rule (multiple policy names can be added in policy_name when they have to moved from one policy_type or device group to another(comma separated). Whereas, for changing their respective position internally in the same device group and policy_type, only one rule can be moved at a time.)

parameters.name: device_group
parameters.description: Device group of the policy rule (up to 31 characters, default is 'shared')

parameters.name: policy_type
parameters.description: Rule base of the policy (pre-rule or post-rule)

parameters.name: dst_device_group
parameters.description: Device group to move the policy rule to (up to 31 characters)

parameters.name: dst_policy_type
parameters.description: Rule base to move the policy to (pre-rule or post-rule)

parameters.name: where
parameters.description: Where to position the policy, by default the policy would be created at the bottom

parameters.name: dst
parameters.description: Policy in reference to which, position the current policy (inter dependent with 'where' parameter, when the value of where is 'before' or 'after', else it would be ignored)

parameters.name: should_commit_changes
parameters.description: Whether to commit both, changes to firewall and changes to device groups at the end of this action

parameters.name: use_partial_commit
parameters.description: Whether to perform user specific commit. As part of the request, the configuration's username is included as the administrator name (when the 'should_commit_changes' is 'false' the 'use_partial_commit' parameter is ignored)

parameters.name: policy_name
parameters.description: Name of the policy rule to delete

parameters.name: policy_type
parameters.description: Rule base to delete the policy from (pre-rule or post-rule)

parameters.name: device_group
parameters.description: Device group of the policy rule (default is 'shared')

parameters.name: should_commit_changes
parameters.description: Whether to commit both, changes to firewall and changes to device groups at the end of this action

parameters.name: use_partial_commit
parameters.description: Whether to perform user specific commit. As part of the request, the configuration's username is included as the administrator name (when the 'should_commit_changes' is 'false' the 'use_partial_commit' parameter is ignored)

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name of the address group to be created (up to 63 characters)

parameters.name: device_group
parameters.description: Device group to create the address group in or 'shared' (up to 31 characters, default is 'shared')

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Type of the address group (Whether to create a static or a dynamic address group)

parameters.name: addresses_or_match_criteria
parameters.description: List of addresses or address_group to add in this address group, if 'type' is static then addresses or address groups to be included in address group have to added and if 'type' is dynamic then the match criteria (using 'and' or 'or' operators with different tags available in that device group, negation is not supported) to add addresses and address groups that satisfy this criteria into this newly created address group has to be added

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Description for the address group (max length 1023 characters)

parameters.name: disable_override
parameters.description: Used to ensure that a firewall administrator cannot override settings locally on a firewall that inherits this configuration through a Device Group commit from Panorama (only used when device group is not 'shared')

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: List of tags to apply to this address group (allow to group objects using keywords or phrases, max length 127 characters)

parameters.name: should_commit_changes
parameters.description: Whether to commit both, changes to firewall and changes to device groups at the end of this action

parameters.name: use_partial_commit
parameters.description: Whether to perform user specific commit. As part of the request, the configuration's username is included as the administrator name (when the 'should_commit_changes' is 'false' the 'use_partial_commit' parameter is ignored)

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name of the address group to be modified

parameters.name: device_group
parameters.description: Device group to which the address group to be modified belongs (default is 'shared')

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Type of the address group

parameters.name: addresses_or_match_criteria
parameters.description: List of addresses or address_group to add in this address group, if 'type' is static then addresses or address groups to be included in address group have to added and if 'type' is dynamic then the match criteria (using 'and' or 'or' operators with different tags available in that device group, negation is not supported) to add addresses and address groups that satisfy this criteria into this newly created address group has to be added

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Description for the address group (max length 1023 characters)

parameters.name: disable_override
parameters.description: Used to ensure that a firewall administrator cannot override settings locally on a firewall that inherits this configuration through a Device Group commit from Panorama (only used when device group is not 'shared')

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: List of tags to apply to this address group (allow to group objects using keywords or phrases, max length 127 characters)

parameters.name: should_commit_changes
parameters.description: Whether to commit both, changes to firewall and changes to device groups at the end of this action

parameters.name: use_partial_commit
parameters.description: Whether to perform user specific commit. As part of the request, the configuration's username is included as the administrator name (when the 'should_commit_changes' is 'false' the 'use_partial_commit' parameter is ignored)

parameters.name: device_group
parameters.description: Device group whose address groups you want to list (up to 31 characters, default is 'shared')

actions.name: Create Address

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Description of address (up to 1023 characters)

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Tags want to apply on an address (comma-separated, up to 127 characters for each tag)

parameters.name: disable_override
parameters.description: Whether to disable override the address or not

parameters.name: use_partial_commit
